description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with a dark coloration that contrasts with the lighter, weathered look of the grooved top. The block appears dense and heavy, likely made from a durable stone such as granite or concrete. Its robust construction suggests it is designed to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly to control pedestrian or vehicular movement. The overall finish is rough and utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, solid sides. The block has a striped, ribbed appearance and a rough, utilitarian finish.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the grooved top, showing the full width of the parallel stripes and the solid, dark side.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes running away from the viewer and the side appearing as a solid dark face.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this view shows the grooved top surface and the opposite side, maintaining the striped pattern.
description_view_3: The grooves are again prominent, with the stripes running away from the viewer and the side face visible as a solid dark surface.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a traffic or pedestrian barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or directly on compacted ground
attribute_weathering_pattern: Some surface discoloration and minor pitting, suggesting exposure to the elements but no significant erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
